name:
American Red Cross
Greater Chesapeake and Potomac
description: 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, the American Red Cross is responding to disasters large and small.

name: Anne Arundel Medical Center
description: Anne Arundel Medical Center's mission is to enhance the health of the people we serve.

name: Art with a Heart
description: Art with a Heart provides hands-on, interactive, and educational visual arts classes in schools, community centers, group homes, shelters, permanent housing facilities, veterans facilities, hospitals, senior facilities, and more.

name: Baer School
description: At the Baer students with multiple disabilities and those who require intensive support are educated through IEP-driven programming.

name: Baltimore Humane Society
description: The Baltimore Humane Society's mission is to care for homeless animals & prevent animal cruelty

name: Baltimore Hunger Project
description: Baltimore Hunger Project mission is to eliminate the growing problem of weekend childhood hunger by feeding bodies and minds.

name:
Baltimore Saints Hockey 
Teresa Zinkhan
description: Baltimore Area Special Hockey, Inc. offers an ice hockey program for both children and adults with a traumatic brain injury or intellectual disability such as Autism and Down Syndrome .

name: Baltimore VA Rehabilitation and Extended Care Center
website: Volunteer Or Donate | VA Maryland Health Care | Veterans Affairswork-with-us/volunteer-or-donate/
description:
To fulfill President Lincoln’s promise “To care for him who shall have borne the battle, and for his widow, and his orphan” by serving and honoring the men and women who are America’s veterans.

VA carries out four specific missions to make good on that commitment.

name: Bedtime in a Box
description: By giving parents and caregivers, especially those in low-income households, the tools to nurture their children, we are developing habits that will last a lifetime, improving early literacy outcomes, and supporting the link between home and school.

name:
Bennett Blazers Sports Program
 at Kennedy Krieger
description: The Bennett Institute Physically Challenged Sports Program and the Bennett Blazers offer programs for children and young adults ages 5-18.

name: Be a Chef for a Day
description: Be A Chef For A Day empowers kids to cultivate healthy, successful futures for themselves and their families through food literacy, culinary competence, curiosity, and creativity

name: Brightwood Center-Genesis Eldercare
description: Brightwood Center-Genesis Eldercare mission is to assist those who are coping with a recuperative skilled nursing or long-term care stay due to illness or injury, and supports center-based services and caregiver education to the enrichment of all.
contact_name: Most Maryland hospitals and medical facilities offer volunteer programs. To learn more, contact your local healthcare provider's Volunteer Services Department. You can reach the Volunteer Services Department by using the provided general contact information.

name: Charm City Lacrosse
description: Charm City Lacrosse elevates the quality of life for individuals with limited mobility by empowering them to train for and participate in mainstream running events.

name: Chesapeake Bay Foundation
description: CBF's mission is to Save the Bay®, and keep it saved.

name: Chesapeake Habitat for Humanity
description: Habitat for Humanity of the Chesapeake brings people together to build homes, communities and hope.

name: Chesapeake Region Accessible Boating
description: Chesapeake Region Accessible Boating mission is to provide the thrill, freedom, and therapeutic value of sailing for anyone with a disability, recovering warriors, and youth from underserved communities.

name: Community Art Center - Art Camp
description: The Community Art Center Summer Spree is a weekly camp running from 9 am – 4 pm for children entering grades Kindergarten – 6th.  Arts activities and experiences form the foundation of our camp. Campers will explore visual arts, ceramics, movement, dramatic play, hiking, music, tie dye, and weekly special projects taught by visiting artists!

name: First Fruits Farm
description: First Fruits Farm is a non-profit, Christian ministry dedicated to providing fresh, nutritious food for the hungry through volunteer service and community partnerships.

name: First Tee
description: We enable kids to build the strength of character that empowers them through a lifetime of new challenges. By seamlessly integrating the game of golf with life skills curriculum, we create learning experiences that build inner strength, self-confidence, and resilience that kids carry to everything they do.

name: GBMC
description: GBMC has offered high school students the opportunity to participate in its student volunteer program for many years. The program is only available during the summer months (June, July and August). Student volunteers gain documented community service hours required for graduation in a positive healthcare setting. For many it is an introduction to ‘the world of work’!
contact_name: Most Maryland hospitals and medical facilities offer volunteer programs. To learn more, contact your local healthcare provider's Volunteer Services Department. You can reach the Volunteer Services Department by using the provided general contact information.

name: Have a Nice Day Project
description: We host social gatherings where we write positive messages on blank coffee c                                                                           Then we donate them to locally owned cafés around Baltimore.

name: Helping Up Mission
description: Helping Up Mission provides hope to people experiencing homelessness, poverty or addiction by meeting their physical, psychological, social and spiritual needs.

name: Happy Helpers
description: We bring love and necessities directly to homeless and poverty-stricken men, women, and children every Saturday and Sunday no matter the weather and including holidays. We also hold many themed parties annually for those whom we serve.

name: Irvine Nature Center
description: The Irvine Nature Center's mission is to teach both children and adults about the importance of conservation, and how we can all work to make our world a better place.

name: Jewish Family Services
description: Jewish Community Services (JCS) is dedicated to providing programs and services that help people of all ages and backgrounds achieve their goals and enhance their well being. JCS, an agency of The Associated: Jewish Federation of Baltimore

name: Living Classrooms
description: Living Classrooms' mission is to provide access to more equitable education, workforce development, community safety, and health and wellness opportunities

name: Maryland Council for Special Equestrians
description: The Maryland Council for Special Equestrians (MCSE) is a nonprofit organization providing equine facilitated therapies to children and adults with disabilities.

name: Maryland Therapeutic Riding
description: Maryland Therapeutic Riding mission is to improve the quality of life of children, adults, active military and veterans with special needs by connecting humans and horses in a healing environment.

name: Meals on Wheels of Central Maryland, Inc.
description: ​MOWCM's mission is to enable people to live independently at home through the provision of nutritious meals, personal contact, and support services, so that they may age in place with dignity.

name:
My Brother’s Keeper
Catholic Charities of Baltimore
description: My Brother’s Keeper began in the Irvington neighborhood of West Baltimore with support with basic services and today offers a wide range of support, from meals to workforce development to youth services.

name: My Sister’s Circle Inc.
description: Sisters Circle is a mentorship and weekly after-school program for incoming sixth grade girls from three BCPSS partner middle schools: Henderson Hopkins, City Springs and Commodore John Rodgers.

name: North Oaks Health Center
description: North Oaks' mission is to enhance the lives of seniors in a resident-centered community through a committed spirit of embracing culture, family and tradition.

name: Our Daily Bread
description: Our Daily Bread Employment Center is Maryland’s largest hot meal program, serving more than a quarter million meals to those experiencing hunger in Baltimore City each year. We serve a nutritious lunch every day of the year and breakfast to seniors and individuals with disabilities each weekday.

name: Paul’s Place
description: Paul's Place mission is to improving the quality of life in Southwest Baltimore by offering a place for individuals and families to nurture their spirits and gain self-sufficiency.

name: Pickersgill Retirement Community
description: Pickersgill Retirement Community is dedicated to helping each person achieve, with dignity, the proper balance between independence and assistance.
contact_name: Most Maryland hospitals and medical facilities offer volunteer programs. To learn more, contact your local healthcare provider's Volunteer Services Department. You can reach the Volunteer Services Department by using the provided general contact information.

name: Playworks
description: To improve the health and well-being of children by increasing opportunities for physical activity and safe, meaningful play.

name: Project Plase
description: Project Plase now has two temporary housing facilities and is in the process of converting an old elementary school in Irvington, Baltimore into affordable housing for 90 homeless individuals.

name:
Reading Partners
Various Elementary Schools in Baltimore City
description: Reading Partners' mission is to help children become lifelong readers by empowering communities to provide individualized instruction with measurable results.

name: Roland Park Place
description: Roland Park Place is the only full-service, accredited not-for-profit Life Plan retirement community in Baltimore City and is a leader in aging services for older adults and their families.

name: Ronald McDonald House
description: RMHC Maryland’s mission is to offer a community of compassion, support, and the comforts of home to families with critically ill children, steps away from the medical care they need.

name: Rose of Sharon Equestrian School Inc
description: Provided by licensed therapists in the authentic farm environment using clinical evidence-based practice.

name: Share Baby
description: ShareBaby's mission is to address the unmet basic needs of Baltimore’s most vulnerable young children through the distribution of diapers and other essential items.

name: Sinai Hospital
description: To improve the health of the individuals and communities we serve through compassionate, high quality care.
contact_name: Most Maryland hospitals and medical facilities offer volunteer programs. To learn more, contact your local healthcare provider's Volunteer Services Department. You can reach the Volunteer Services Department by using the provided general contact information.

name: Special Olympics
description: Special Olympics' mission is to provide year-round sports training and athletic competition in a variety of Olympic-type sports for children and adults with intellectual disabilities. This gives them continuing opportunities to develop physical fitness, demonstrate courage, experience joy and participate in a sharing of gifts, skills and friendship with their families, other Special Olympics athletes and the community.

name: Springhouse Assisted Living
description: Springhouse Assisted Living is an assisted living community designed specifically to provide supportive care for seniors.
contact_name: Most Maryland hospitals and medical facilities offer volunteer programs. To learn more, contact your local healthcare provider's Volunteer Services Department. You can reach the Volunteer Services Department by using the provided general contact information.

name: St. Vincent de Paul Baltimore
description: Leading provider of community service to people suffering from homelessness & hunger

name: The Arc Baltimore
description: The Arc Baltimore's mission is to support people with developmental disabilities to lead fulfilling lives with a sense of belonging, purpose, and meaningful relationships.

name: The Arc of Howard County
description: The Arc of Howard County's mission is to achieve full community life for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities — one person at a time.

name: The Baltimore Station
description: The Baltimore Station is an innovative therapeutic residential and outpatient treatment program supporting veterans who are overcoming obstacles to regain self-sufficiency.

name:
The Believe in Tomorrow Children’s House
At Johns Hopkins Hospital
description: Children’s House at Johns Hopkins' mission is to provide a family-centered supportive residence for children being treated for life threatening illness and their families.

name: The Family Tree
description: The Family Tree is the leading authority in Maryland for the prevention of child abuse and neglect.

name: The Friendship Circle
description:
The Friendship Circle exists to bring happiness and companionship to children and young adults with special needs by celebrating their individuality, as well as bringing energy, support, and peace of mind to their families.

​

name: Village Learning Place
description: The mission of the Village Learning Place is to promote literacy, cultural awareness, and lifelong learning through access for all ages to information, resources, and educational programs.
